what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG outline icon showing one top node connected to three lower nodes, representing a hierarchy or organizational tree.
observations: The SVG uses a 24 by 24 viewBox with explicit width and height of 24.
It has no fill and uses currentColor for its stroke.
Stroke styling is set to 1.5 width with round line caps and joins.
Four small rounded rectangles form the hierarchy nodes.
A single path draws the vertical and horizontal connectors between nodes.
standouts: Represents the structure with simple rectangles and connector lines.
Keeps the icon self-contained without ids, classes, or embedded styles.
conventions: Uses inline SVG geometry with rect and path elements.
Follows a monochrome stroke-only icon pattern.
Uses small rounded corners on node rectangles.
Uses explicit presentation attributes rather than CSS classes.
notes: A compact hierarchy icon built from four node rectangles and a connector path.
questions: Should the fixed width and height remain on the SVG, or should sizing be delegated to consumers?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a hierarchy icon, and the SVG content depicts a hierarchical node diagram.
intention_signals: It appears intended as a reusable UI icon for hierarchy, organization chart, tree, or parent-child relationship concepts.
novelty: The branching connector is encoded as one compact path while the nodes remain separate rect elements.
contribution: Provides a reusable currentColor outline asset for visually expressing hierarchical relationships in a fused interface or design system.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 stroke-only SVG hierarchy icon made of four rounded rectangles connected by a branching line path.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing hierarchical or organizational structure as a reusable monochrome UI icon.
